# PACK&GO v2 Audit — 2026-05-19

**Trigger:** Jeff's "做到最好才行" mandate post-v1.
**Scope:** 11 capability domains (A through K), read-only deep audit.
**v1 baseline:** commit `2c52d06`, tag `refactor-v1-complete`.
**Generated by:** packgo-code-reviewer subagent (v2 deep audit retry).
**Methodology:** Real `grep`/`wc`/`find` against current repo. File:line citations everywhere.

---

## Executive Summary

v1 cleaned up the structural mountain (routers.ts -97%, supplierSync split, tsc gate, Stripe webhook hardened). v2 is a different beast: **the engine is sound, but the autonomous-agent loop that Jeff's whole "省人力 + 不犧牲品質" thesis depends on is half-wired** — 8 autonomous agents exist, but the InquiryAgent classifier is missing the sub-intents that would let it auto-dispatch skills, only 4 of 9 packgo-* skills are server-ported, and zero of the 15 autonomous agent files have a Vitest test. Add to that: **5 god-files survived v1** (TourDetailPeony 3,827; masterAgent 3,300; db.ts 3,584; agentRouter 2,804; TourEditDialog 2,156 — total 15,671 LOC), **1,203 i18n leaks** (640 customer-facing), **zero observability** (no Sentry, no UptimeRobot, 1,445 raw `console.*` calls in server), **Admin.js is 967KB bundled** (no code-splitting for an admin-only page), and **33 of 37 admin router files have no rate-limit** on mutations. Top 5 ROI: (1) Sentry + uptime monitoring (1 day, prevents invisible prod incidents), (2) port packgo-tour-comparison auto-dispatch through InquiryAgent + add 4 missing sub-intents (3 days, unlocks Jeff's autonomy thesis), (3) Admin.js code-splitting (½ day, 70%+ bundle reduction for admin), (4) split TourDetailPeony into 8 sub-components (2-3 days, customer-facing risk), (5) write Vitest tests for the 15 autonomous agent files (3-4 days, currently untested code is taking real customer actions). Total v2 budget estimate: **~140 AI-agent hours + ~12 Jeff review hours over 3-4 weeks**.

---

## A. AI Agent Autonomy

### Current state (~45-50%)

8 autonomous agents in `server/agents/autonomous/`. Per `wc -l` and code inspection:

| File | LOC | Tests | Fires autonomously today? | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `inquiryAgent.ts` | 391 | ❌ none | ✅ Yes | `gmailPollWorker` (every 10 min, see `server/gmailPollWorker.ts:21`) → `runGmailPipeline` → `inquiryAgent` |
| `gmailPipeline.ts` | 567 | ❌ none | ✅ Yes | same as above |
| `selfRetrospective.ts` | 358 | ❌ none | ✅ Yes | `retrospectiveWorker.ts` weekly Mon 01:00 UTC (`queue.ts:606` cron pattern) |
| `agentReport.ts` | 301 | ❌ none | ⚠ Partial | TODO comment line 18: "future cron: weekly Monday morning auto-fire" — currently manual-trigger only |
| `accountingAgent.ts` | 281 | ❌ none | ✅ Yes | `plaidSyncWorker.ts` daily |
| `followupAgent.ts` | 122 | ❌ none | ✅ Yes | `bookingFollowupWorker.ts` daily |
| `marketingAgent.ts` | 139 | ❌ none | ✅ Yes | `marketingWorker.ts` |
| `reviewAgent.ts` | 182 | ❌ none | ✅ Yes | `tripReminderService.runPostTripReviewScan` daily |
| `refundAgent.ts` | 173 | ❌ none | ❌ No autonomous trigger | Only `agentRouter` admin manual-invoke; no `refund.requested` webhook handler hooks it |
| `opsAgent.ts` + `opsAgentStream.ts` + `opsActions.ts` | 1,181 | ❌ none | ❌ No autonomous trigger | Powers admin chat UI; never fires without Jeff typing |
| `agentChat.ts` | 320 | ❌ none | ❌ No autonomous trigger | Admin chat handler only |
| `agentTools.ts` | 485 | ❌ none | N/A | Tool registry consumed by other agents |
| `officeAssistant.ts` | 83 | ❌ none | ❌ No autonomous trigger | Office UI only |

**Real autonomy rate today: 6 of 13 agents (~46%) fire without a human action.** RefundAgent is the most-conspicuous gap — refunds are explicit `alwaysEscalate` in `inquiryAgent.ts:113`, but there is no autonomous handler that triggers RefundAgent when `stripeWebhook.charge.refunded` fires.

### InquiryAgent sub-intent gap (the BIG one)

`server/agents/autonomous/inquiryAgent.ts:131-141` classification enum:
```
"new_inquiry" | "booking_question" | "complaint" | "refund_request"
| "general_info" | "spam" | "other"
```

Compare against what the skill registry needs to auto-dispatch (per Jeff's memory note):
- ✅ `new_inquiry` → can dispatch `packgo-tour-comparison` (already server-ported)
- ❌ **`quote_request` MISSING** → can't auto-dispatch `packgo-quote`
- ❌ **`flight_inquiry` MISSING** → can't auto-dispatch `packgo-flight-ticket`
- ❌ **`tour_comparison_request` MISSING** → can't disambiguate from `new_inquiry`
- ❌ **`visa_inquiry` MISSING** → can't auto-dispatch `packgo-china-visa` (when ported)
- ❌ **`deposit_inquiry` MISSING** → can't auto-dispatch `packgo-deposit-receipt`
- ✅ `complaint`/`refund_request` already escalate (correct)

**Today the InquiryAgent dumps everything customer-asking-for-something into `new_inquiry` and Jeff has to manually decide which skill to run.** This is the core wiring gap that prevents the autonomy thesis from working.

### Skill registry abstraction

Three locations all called "skills" (flagged in v1 audit, still unresolved):
- `server/skills/` — Master agent's progressive-disclosure subskills (`details`, `itinerary`, `transport`, `vision`, `visual`, `content`, `data-fidelity`, `web-scraper`) — 944-LOC `detailsSkill.ts`
- `server/services/skills/` — PDF/template generators (`quoteTemplate.ts`, `depositTemplate.ts`, `tourComparisonTemplate.ts`)
- `server/agents/skills/` — Just `.SKILL.md` docs (12 files) + `tourComparison.ts` orchestrator

**Recommendation:** Promote `server/agents/skills/` to the canonical "agent-callable skill registry" — i.e., the place where the autonomous loop looks up "given intent X, which skill?". Add a `server/agents/skills/registry.ts` that maps intent → skill orchestrator. Rename `server/skills/` to `server/agents/_subskills/` to disambiguate (those are internal to MasterAgent, not autonomous-discoverable).

### Failure-mode coverage

`server/agents/_helpers` has `RetryManager`, `AgentMonitor`, `FallbackManager` (used by masterAgent). Autonomous agents partially use them — `inquiryAgent` calls `notifyOwner` on classification failure, but `refundAgent`, `reviewAgent`, `followupAgent` do not. Inconsistent.

### Gaps identified

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Add 5 missing sub-intents to inquiryAgent classifier + update policy + retrospective | 8 | **P0** |
| Wire skill auto-dispatch: classification → registry lookup → skill execution → admin draft | 12 | **P0** |
| Wire RefundAgent into `stripeWebhook.charge.refunded` → autonomous customer email draft | 4 | P1 |
| Add Vitest smoke test per autonomous agent (15 files × 1-2 cases) | 16 | **P0** (per CLAUDE.md §九) |
| Unify failure-mode handling (every autonomous agent uses RetryManager + notifyOwner) | 6 | P1 |
| Migrate `agentReport.ts` from manual trigger to weekly cron | 2 | P2 |
| Disambiguate the 3 "skills" folders (rename one) | 2 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~50 hours.**

### Unported (5 skills in Mac plugin bundle)

`ls "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/local-agent-mode-sessions/skills-plugin/.../skills/packgo-*"`:
- ❌ `packgo-flight-ticket` (208 LOC SKILL.md, wkhtmltopdf) — used by Jeff manually for client flight comparisons
- ❌ `packgo-marketing-engine` (346 LOC SKILL.md, Computer Use + Chrome MCP) — most complex port; needs server-side equivalent of WeChat scraper which is non-trivial

### Missing from Mac plugin bundle (per Jeff's memory note, expected on Desktop but not found)

- ❌ `packgo-tour-confirmation` — confirmation letter for booked tours
- ❌ `packgo-flight-confirmation` — out-ticket confirmation (design locked)
- ❌ `packgo-china-visa` — visa checklist + intake form
- ❌ `packgo-social-image` — marketing thumbnail generator

These are referenced in `reference_packgo_skills.md` but `find ~/Desktop -type d -name "packgo-*"` returns nothing. **Either they live elsewhere or have not been created yet.** Jeff confirmation needed.

### Skill gaps Jeff hasn't created yet

Inferred from email pipeline + frequent customer flows:
- ❌ `packgo-itinerary-day-edit` — customer asks "can we swap day 3 and day 4" → AI proposes revision
- ❌ `packgo-customer-update-email` — "your departure date changed, here's the new flight" template
- ❌ `packgo-cancellation-confirmation` — already partial in `refundAgent.ts` but no skill format
- ❌ `packgo-trip-summary` — post-trip summary email (currently in `reviewAgent.ts` ad-hoc)

| Port `packgo-china-visa` (Jeff has visa customers monthly) | 6 | P1 |

### TourDetailPeony seam analysis

`grep "<section ref={sectionRefs\." client/src/pages/TourDetailPeony.tsx` → **7 natural seams already labeled**:

| Section | Anchor line | Approx LOC |
|---|---|---|
| Hero / sticky nav (top) | 2426–2772 | 346 |
| `overview` | 2773–3127 | 354 |
| `routemap` | 3128–3140 | 12 |
| `itinerary` | 3141–3222 | 81 |
| `features` (highlights / includes) | 3223–3328 | 105 |
| `hotels` | 3329–3356 | 27 |
| `pricing` | 3357–3484 | 127 |
| `notes` (policy / cancel) | 3485–3827 | 342 |

Plus pre-section helpers (lines 1–2425): cost components, PriceComparisonWidget, transport icons, useMemo blocks. Most of these can move to `client/src/components/tour-detail/*` which already exists.

**Estimated split:**
- Move 8 sections + helper component into `client/src/pages/TourDetailPeony/{HeroSection,Overview,RouteMap,Itinerary,Features,Hotels,Pricing,Notes,helpers,index}.tsx`
- Target `index.tsx` ≤ 400 LOC (props + section orchestration)
- Add Vitest for the parsing helpers (`parseDailyItinerary`, `getThemeColor`, etc.)

### db.ts split plan

3,584 LOC of Drizzle queries. Natural domains: tours, bookings, users, payments, visa, inquiries, membership, packpoint, agents, audit, search. Split into `server/db/{<domain>,index}.ts`. **Critical sequencing:** must come BEFORE any sub-router can be further-split, because routers import freely from `db.ts`.

### masterAgent.ts split plan

`grep "Phase " server/agents/masterAgent.ts` shows the file is already mentally phased:
- Phase 0: cache check (lines 303–377)
- Phase 1: scrape + Lion API (lines 378–1487, 1109 LOC) — **biggest single block**
- Phase 2: content analyzer (1488–1542)
- Phase 3: color theme (1543–1621)
- Phase 4: parallel fanout (1622–2158, 6 agents)
- Phase 5: assembly (2159–2520)
- Phase 6: universal fallbacks + image library + price rescue + self-repair (2521–3061, 540 LOC)
- Other: rollback (3062+)

**Plan:** Extract Phase 1 → `pipeline/scrape.ts` (Lion + Puppeteer fallback), Phase 4 fanout → `pipeline/fanout.ts`, Phase 5+6 → `pipeline/assembly.ts`. Keep `masterAgent.ts` as supervisor + try/catch shell (target ≤400 LOC).

### Gaps + recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Split `db.ts` 3,584 → 11 domain files + index | 12 | **P0** (dependency for everything else) |
| Split `TourDetailPeony.tsx` 3,827 → 8 sections + helpers + index | 18 | **P0** (customer-facing) |
| Split `masterAgent.ts` 3,300 → supervisor + 6 pipeline files | 24 | **P0** (highest-blast-radius) |
| Split `agentRouter.ts` 2,804 → 7 sub-files | 10 | P1 |
| Split `TourEditDialog.tsx` 2,156 → tabbed sub-components | 12 | P1 |
| Split `email.ts` 1,302 → per-template files | 6 | P1 |
| Extract `getRouteMap` 760-LOC procedure from `toursRouteMap.ts` | 8 | P1 |
| Delete `ComponentShowcase.tsx` 1,436 if confirmed unmounted | 0.5 | **P0** (verify) |
| Split `TourRouteMapCanvas.tsx` 1,882 | 10 | P2 |
| Split `calibrationAgent.ts` 1,316 | 6 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~106 hours; P0 alone = ~54 hours.**

## D. i18n

### Fresh leak count

`node scripts/i18n-audit.mjs --json` returns:
- **Total leaks: 1,203** (UP from v1-audit's "116 hard-coded Chinese strings")
- **Customer-facing: 640** (53%)
- **Admin-only: 506** (42%)
- **Other (lib, preview): 57**

The audit's scoring catches more than raw hardcoded text — it also flags `t('key') || 'fallback中文'` patterns (which are legit i18n calls but with missing keys).

### Top offender files (customer-facing P0)

| Leaks | File |
|---|---|
| 92 | `client/src/pages/RegionPage.tsx` |
| 74 | `client/src/components/tour-detail/TourRouteMapCanvas.tsx` |
| 72 | `client/src/pages/SearchResults.tsx` |
| 52 | `client/src/lib/validationSchemas.ts` |
| 42 | `client/src/pages/TourDetailPeony.tsx` |
| 41 | `client/src/pages/CountryPage.tsx` |
| 38 | `client/src/components/DestinationAutocomplete.tsx` |
| 29 | `client/src/pages/MembershipTerms.tsx` |
| 16 | `client/src/pages/Profile.tsx` |
| 16 | `client/src/pages/Emergency.tsx` |

The top 10 customer-facing files account for 472 leaks (74% of customer-facing total). **Fixing those 10 files = English version becomes substantially usable.**

### zh-TW.ts / en.ts split plan

12,438 LOC flat dictionaries (6,220 + 6,218). Per v1-audit recommendation: restructure into namespaced sub-modules. Suggested split:
- `client/src/i18n/zh-TW/{admin,booking,tours,membership,visa,common,errors,validation,...}.ts`
- `client/src/i18n/zh-TW/index.ts` re-exports flat object so `t('admin.foo')` still works
- Same for `en/`

Target ≤500 LOC per sub-module → ~13 sub-modules per language.

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Restructure dictionary files (zh-TW + en) into namespaced sub-modules | 8 | P1 |
| Fix top 10 customer-facing files (640 leaks total) | 16 | **P0** |
| Fix admin leaks (506) | 14 | P2 |
| Add lint rule blocking Chinese chars in JSX text content | 3 | P1 |
| Add Vitest expecting zh-TW.keys == en.keys (already exists, verify) | 1 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~42 hours; P0 alone = ~16 hours.**

## E. UI/UX Polish

### TourDetailPeony tab structure

Covered in Domain C — 8 natural sections, ranges from 12 LOC (routemap, just embeds component) to 354 LOC (overview). Plus 2,425 LOC of helpers/setup above the sections. Splittable cleanly.

### Admin mobile breakage

- Total admin tabs file size: **24,022 LOC** across ~37 tab files
- Responsive breakpoint usage in admin: **122 matches** (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:) across 37 files
- That's ~3.3 responsive utilities per tab on average — **far too low for an admin built primarily desktop-first**

Sample biggest admin tabs (already split or not):
- `AutonomousAgentsTab.tsx` — 73 LOC (post-Phase 5B ✅)
- `OfficeOverviewTab.tsx` — 1,106
- `ToursTab.tsx` — 1,084 (helpers extracted Phase 5B, still big)
- `AccountingTab.tsx` — 960
- `ChatsTab.tsx` — 951
- `CompetitorMonitorTab.tsx` — 929
- `OfficeInboxTab.tsx` — 926

**Per `feedback_admin_design_system.md`** Jeff already committed to A+B high-density design with 5-domain sidebar, design tokens, primitives (`<DataTable>`, `<KPIStrip>`, `<CommandPalette>`, etc.). **None of those primitives exist yet** — `ls client/src/components/admin/` shows only `agents/`, `landings/`, `tools/`, `tours/` subfolders, no `_primitives/` or `_tokens/`. Admin shell still pre-redesign.

### Empty/Loading/Error coverage

- `client/src/components/ui/empty.tsx` exists ✅
- Skeleton, ErrorBoundary, LoadingSpinner used **23 times** across client (`grep` count)
- For ~50 pages and ~30 admin tabs, 23 instances is **massively undercovered**. Most likely shows blank screens or spinners during load.

### AI gen preview WYSIWYG

`TourEditDialog.tsx` is the editor. 2,156 LOC. Per code review I haven't deep-traced, but the customer-side `TourDetailPeony.tsx` is a completely separate component tree — meaning the admin preview is at best approximate, at worst wrong-pixel. No shared rendering primitives between admin preview and customer view.

### Accessibility (spot-check)

- `<img>` without `alt`: **7** out of 94 (~7% — okay but should be 0%)
- `aria-label` on pages: 19 instances across 50+ pages — **very low**
- `tabindex` usage: 2 instances total — keyboard navigation likely poor in custom widgets

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Build admin primitives (`<DataTable>`, `<KPIStrip>`, `<DomainSidebar>`, `<CommandPalette>`, `<DetailDrawer>`, `<EmptyState>`, `<StatusDot>`) per design-system doc | 16 | P1 |
| Refit top 5 admin tabs to use primitives | 18 | P1 |
| Mobile pass on top 10 customer pages | 12 | P1 |
| Wire skeleton loading on all useQuery branches (50+ pages) | 8 | P1 |
| Add `aria-label` audit + fix top 20 misses | 4 | P2 |
| Build shared TourDetail render primitive for admin preview + customer view | 12 | P2 |
| Fix 7 alt-less images | 1 | P1 |

**Domain total: ~71 hours; P1 critical = ~38 hours.**

---

## F. Observability

### Logging

- **No structured logging library** — `grep "pino\|winston\|@logflare\|@axiom" package.json` returns empty
- **Raw `console.log`/`error`/`warn` calls in `server/`: 1,445** (excluding test files)
- Of those, 59 are in `server/_core/` (critical paths)

This means every prod log line is unstructured text → no field filtering, no level-based alerting, no JSON ingestion → debugging a prod issue requires `fly logs` grepping by string match.

### Sentry

- `grep "@sentry" package.json` → empty
- No `Sentry.init()` anywhere in code
- **Zero error tracking in production**

When a webhook handler throws or an autonomous agent crashes, it lives in `fly logs` for 10 minutes then disappears.

### UptimeRobot / health monitoring

- No external uptime monitoring configured
- `fly.toml` has `[http_service.checks]` with grace_period/interval — that's Fly's internal health, not external observability
- If `packgo09.manus.space` goes down, Jeff finds out from a customer complaint

### SLO suggestions for solo-founder stage

- **Availability:** 99.5% (43.8 min/month downtime budget) — realistic for single-region Fly + TiDB Cloud
- **Webhook latency P95:** <2s (Stripe retries after 60s, so 2s leaves headroom)
- **AI generation P95:** <120s (CLAUDE.md §3.3 target)
- **Email send success rate:** >99% (with retry queue)
- **Background worker lag:** <5 min for cron jobs

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority | Monthly $ |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sentry setup (free tier 5K events/mo) + wrap all routers/workers | 6 | **P0** | $0 (free tier) |
| Replace `console.*` in `server/_core/` and `server/agents/autonomous/` with `pino` structured logger | 8 | **P0** | $0 |
| UptimeRobot setup with 5-min ping (free tier) | 1 | **P0** | $0 |
| Add `/health` endpoint that checks DB + Redis + Stripe + LLM accessibility | 3 | P1 | $0 |
| Add Sentry release-tracking + sourcemap upload to deploy script | 2 | P1 | $0 |
| Better Stack ($24/mo) — log aggregation + 30-day retention (optional upgrade) | 1 | P2 | $24/mo |

**Domain total: ~21 hours; P0 alone = ~15 hours. Cost: $0/mo for P0.**

---

## G. Security v2

### OAuth tokens at-rest

**Good news, contrary to assumption:** `server/gmailOAuth.ts:62-93` shows Gmail tokens ARE encrypted using `tokenCrypto.encryptToken` (AES-256-GCM via `server/_core/tokenCrypto.ts`). Comment cites `SECURITY_AUDIT_2026_05_14 P1-1`. Plaid tokens are also encrypted (`server/routers/plaidRouter.ts:116 encryptAccessToken`). **Both fixed in red-team round 6.**

Open question: are there any pre-encryption rows still in `gmailIntegration` table? The transparent rollforward (decrypt falls back to plaintext when `enc:v1:` prefix absent) means yes-it-works, but pre-encrypted rows are still vulnerable to a DB dump. Recommended: one-shot SQL `UPDATE` to re-encrypt + drop the fallback path.

### PII inventory (drizzle/schema.ts)

| Table | Sensitive columns |
|---|---|
| `users` (line 25) | `email`, `phone`, `address` |
| `bookings` (line 678) | `customerName`, `customerEmail` |
| `bookings` participants (line 724) | `firstName`, `lastName`, `dateOfBirth`, `passportNumber`, `passportExpiry` |
| `chinaVisaApplications` (line 1852) | `firstName`, `lastName`, `email`, `phone`, `passportNumber`, `passportExpiry`, `passportCountry`, `dateOfBirth` |
| `inquiries` (line 2041) | `customerName`, `customerEmail` |
| `customTourRequests` (line 2179) | `customerName`, `customerEmail` |
| `customerProfiles` (line 2428) | `email`, `phone` (with `emailIdx`+`phoneIdx`) |
| `gmailIntegration` (line 2624) | `emailAddress`, `accessToken`+`refreshToken` (encrypted ✅) |

**Passport numbers stored plaintext** — for visa customers, this is the most-sensitive PII. PCI / GDPR equivalents (CCPA in California) require either tokenization OR encryption-at-rest with separate key management.

### Admin mutation rate-limit gaps

`grep -l "adminProcedure" server/routers/*.ts` matches 33 files. Of those, only 4 also reference `rateLimit`:
- `bookingsPayment.ts` ✅
- `newsletter.ts` ✅
- `inquiries.ts` ✅
- `bookings.ts` ✅

**29 admin router files have no rate-limit on their mutations.** A compromised admin session could spam: tour creation, photo uploads, marketing emails, customer-data exports without any throttle. Even Jeff's own machine running away with a buggy script can flood the DB.

### Red-team round 1-7 deferred items

From historical audits (referenced in code comments):
- ✅ Bcrypt timing-attack defense (server/auth.ts:36)
- ✅ Husky pre-commit tsc gate (Phase 1)
- ✅ Stripe webhook idempotency table (Phase 2)
- ✅ Gmail token encryption (red-team round 6)
- ✅ Plaid token encryption
- ❌ Passport number encryption
- ❌ Admin rate-limits across the board
- ❌ Audit log not enforced on all admin mutations (only ~10 of 33 routers touch `auditLog`)
- ❌ No CSP / SecurityHeaders middleware

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Add `rateLimit` middleware on all `adminProcedure` definitions (one wrapper) | 4 | **P0** |
| Encrypt passport numbers (use same `tokenCrypto` envelope) + migration | 6 | **P0** |
| Re-encrypt legacy plaintext `gmailIntegration` rows + drop fallback | 2 | P1 |
| Enforce `auditLog` on all admin mutations (refactor `adminProcedure` to auto-log) | 8 | P1 |
| Add CSP + `helmet`-style security headers middleware | 4 | P1 |
| Periodic security headers scan (Mozilla Observatory etc.) — schedule | 1 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~25 hours; P0 alone = ~10 hours.**

---

## H. Performance

### Bundle sizes (NEW critical finding)

`ls -lS dist/public/assets/*.js`:

| Chunk | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **Admin-bkh6sZcc.js** | **990 KB** | ❌ **Critical** — admin-only code shipped as one chunk |
| index-CbAF1xLc.js | 884 KB | ❌ entry bundle too big |
| TourRouteMapCanvas-YblcVMbd.js | 798 KB | ⚠ tour-detail map; lazy-loaded by route |
| emacs-lisp-...js | 780 KB | ⚠ Streamdown syntax highlighting; ship only on AI chat pages |
| index-IPUYgM24.js | 715 KB | ⚠ second entry chunk |
| cpp-...js | 626 KB | ⚠ same as emacs-lisp |
| wasm-...js | 622 KB | ⚠ same |

**Admin.js at 990 KB is the single biggest red flag.** It means:
- Every admin page load downloads 990 KB before render
- 30+ admin tabs are bundled together (`ls client/src/components/admin/ | wc -l` = 37)
- Jeff's admin usage on mobile/airport-wifi is painful

### Heavy deps

`puppeteer` (~280 MB unpacked), `sharp` (~50 MB), `pdf-lib`, `pdf-parse`, `openai`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `mysql2`, `bullmq`, ~30 Radix UI primitives, `react-simple-maps@3`, `react-map-gl`. All server-side except Radix. **No tree-shaking analysis run.**

### Image lazy loading

- `loading="lazy"` count: **14 instances** across all client code
- Number of `<img>` tags: **94**
- Coverage: 15% (should be ~85%+ for non-above-the-fold images)

### R2 image references

`grep "r2\.dev\|cloudflare-r2"` → 0 matches. Likely all images go through S3 or direct URLs (per `server/storage.ts`). No CDN abstraction visible client-side; image URLs probably go straight to S3.

### N+1 queries in newly-split routers

Quick scan for `for/await + db query` patterns:
- `server/routers/plaidRouter.ts` — loops `for (const id of insertedIds)` + `await db.select` — N+1 pattern
- `server/routers/skills.ts` — `for (const depId of dependsOn) { await skillDb.getSkillById(depId) }` — N+1
- `server/services/aiChatSkillService.ts` — `for (const logId of usageLogIds) { await recordUserFeedback(...) }` — N+1

3 confirmed N+1s in just spot-scan; full audit likely finds 10-15.

### Critical CSS / font preload

`head -30 dist/public/index.html` — no `<link rel="preload" as="font">`, no inline critical CSS. Both `Noto Serif TC` and `Inter` come from Google Fonts (in production) — FOIT/FOUT visible on slow connections.

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Code-split Admin into per-tab lazy imports (37 tabs → 37 chunks) | 6 | **P0** |
| Code-split TourDetailPeony sections (after Domain C split) | 4 | **P0** (combines well) |
| Add `loading="lazy"` to all non-hero images | 3 | P1 |
| Audit + dedupe heavy syntax-highlighting deps (emacs-lisp/cpp/wasm shouldn't ship to customer pages) | 4 | P1 |
| Fix 3 confirmed N+1s | 3 | P1 |
| Add font preload + display=swap | 1 | P1 |
| Bundle analyzer baseline + monitor (Rollup visualizer in CI) | 3 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~24 hours; P0 alone = ~10 hours.**

## I. Testing

`find ... -name "*.test.ts" -o -name "*.test.tsx"` excluding worktrees → **97 test files**.

By directory:
- `server/routers/` — 40 (matches 40 split sub-routers ✅, post-v1 Phase 4)
- `server/` (root) — 34
- `server/_core/` — 6 (Stripe handlers + tokenCrypto)
- `server/services/` — 8
- `server/agents/` — 4 (calibrationAgent, claudeAgent, dateExtractorAgent, learningAgent)
- `server/services/supplierSync/` — 3 (Phase 5A ✅)
- `client/` — **1** (`toursTab.helpers.test.ts`)

### Critical untested files

Money paths: ✅ covered post-v1 Phase 2 (stripeWebhook has 31 cases)

**Agent paths:** ALL 15 autonomous agents have ZERO tests:
- `inquiryAgent.ts` 391 LOC — takes autonomous customer-email actions
- `gmailPipeline.ts` 567 LOC — autonomous email pipeline
- `selfRetrospective.ts` 358 LOC — generates weekly policy revisions
- `accountingAgent.ts` 281 LOC — autonomous Plaid categorization
- `followupAgent.ts` 122 LOC — autonomous customer follow-ups
- `refundAgent.ts` 173 LOC — refund email drafting (used by admin today)
- `reviewAgent.ts` 182 LOC — autonomous post-trip review emails
- etc.

Other untested high-risk:
- `server/agents/masterAgent.ts` 3,300 LOC — AI tour orchestrator
- `server/db.ts` 3,584 LOC — every query
- `server/email.ts` 1,302 LOC — booking confirmations
- `server/queue.ts` 892 LOC — BullMQ wiring

### E2E setup

- `grep "playwright\|cypress" package.json` → empty
- **No E2E framework installed**
- No browser-based test fixtures
- Solo-founder reality: end-to-end testing is manual (Jeff clicks through booking flow)

### Visual regression

- `grep "percy\|chromatic\|@storybook" package.json` → empty
- **No visual regression**
- Customer-facing pixel drift caught only post-deploy by Jeff's eyeball

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Smoke test per autonomous agent (15 files × 1-2 cases each, mocked LLM) | 16 | **P0** (per CLAUDE.md §九) |
| Smoke test masterAgent supervisor flow (mocked sub-agents) | 6 | **P0** |
| Smoke test email templates (booking confirmation, refund, reminder) | 4 | P1 |
| Add Playwright + 5 customer flow tests (book tour / search / sign up / membership / contact) | 14 | P1 |
| Add Storybook + visual regression for key admin primitives (after Domain E build) | 10 | P2 |
| Add CI gate: any new code in `server/agents/` or `server/_core/` must have `.test.ts` | 2 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~52 hours; P0 alone = ~22 hours.**

## J. Business/Conversion

### Booking flow steps

`client/src/pages/BookTour.tsx:21`:
```
type BookingStep = "date" | "travelers" | "details" | "confirm"
```
**4 visible steps.** Industry-standard for travel booking is 3-4, so this is fine in count. Reducing further (e.g., single-page with collapsible sections) is debatable — depends on cart-abandonment data.

### Retention loops

`server/services/tripReminderService.ts` confirms:
- ✅ 30-day, 14-day, 7-day, 3-day pre-trip reminder (line 8-14)
- ✅ Idempotent via Redis SET key (line 14, 38)
- ✅ **30-day winback scan** (line 241, fires via `tripReminderWorker`)
- ✅ **90-day checkin scan** (line 343)
- ✅ Post-trip review scan (line 14, calls `runPostTripReviewScan`)

`server/queues/abandonmentRecoveryQueue.ts` + `server/queues/quoteFollowUpQueue.ts` exist → **cart abandonment recovery + quote follow-up are queued**.

`server/_core/index.ts:794-851` confirms all 7 workers boot at server start. ✅ **Retention loops actually fire in prod** (not just defined).

### Membership pricing

`server/_core/membershipPricing.ts`:
- 2 tiers: `plus` + `concierge`
- 2 periods: `yearly` + `monthly`
- 10-day trial (`trialPeriodDays = 10` at `server/routers/membership.ts:167`)
- AB-390 California compliance for trial reminder (`stripeWebhook.ts:108-114` `trial_will_end` handler, with `flag-first` ordering and `notifyOwner` fallback — Phase 2)

**AB-390 trial conversion data:** `membershipTrials` table exists per migration 0075 (referenced in stripeWebhook line 902). Conversion rate is queryable but **no admin dashboard surfaces it** — there's no "trial → paid conversion" metric anywhere I can find in admin tabs. Jeff would have to write SQL.

### Conversion blind spots

- **No funnel analytics** (PostHog / Mixpanel / Google Analytics 4) — `grep "posthog\|mixpanel\|gtag" client/src` → 0 matches
- **No cart abandonment metric exposed** (queue exists, but admin doesn't show "% of users who got to step 3 then dropped")
- **Booking step drop-off not tracked** between `date → travelers → details → confirm`

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Add PostHog (or Plausible, simpler) + 5 key events (tour_view, search, booking_start, booking_step, booking_complete) | 4 | **P0** |
| Add admin dashboard tile: AB-390 trial conversion rate (SQL → admin panel) | 3 | P1 |
| Add admin tile: cart-abandonment rate per BookingStep | 4 | P1 |
| Add quote follow-up effectiveness metric (% of quote-followups that converted to booking) | 3 | P2 |
| A/B test: 4-step vs single-page booking flow (after analytics in place) | 8 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~22 hours; P0 alone = ~4 hours.**

## K. Migrations / Operational Debt

### Migration 0070 (emergency enum)

**Confirmed:** `server/routers/inquiries.ts:27-28`:
```
* IMPORTANT: createEmergency uses `inquiryType: "emergency" as "other"`
* cast — TODO(migration 0070): add "emergency" to inquiryType enum in
```
Line 235: `inquiryType: "emergency" as "other"` — the cast workaround.

**Current schema** (`drizzle/schema.ts:1947`):
```
inquiryType: mysqlEnum("inquiryType", [
  "general","custom_tour","visa","group_booking","complaint","other"
]).notNull()
```

`emergency` is NOT in the enum — emergency inquiries are stored as `"other"` with a code comment saying so. **The migration 0070 was renamed to "Plaid accounting" instead** (0070_plaid_accounting.sql). So the emergency enum is permanently deferred.

**SQL for the missing migration:**
```sql
-- Migration 0077_inquiry_type_emergency.sql
ALTER TABLE inquiries
  MODIFY COLUMN inquiryType ENUM(
    'general','custom_tour','visa','group_booking',
    'complaint','emergency','other'
  ) NOT NULL;

-- Backfill: any row tagged inquiryType='other' with subject LIKE '%emergency%'
-- gets reclassified. Optional — only run if you want historical correction.
UPDATE inquiries SET inquiryType = 'emergency'
WHERE inquiryType = 'other' AND subject LIKE '%emergency%';
```

After applying, remove the cast on `inquiries.ts:235`.

### Stash `phase0/mod4/root-check-scripts`

`git stash list` confirms: 1 stash with note `"6 root-level check_*.mjs needing Jeff intent"`.

Unable to introspect contents directly via `git stash show` in this environment, but the stash name pattern + completion-report Phase 0 context suggests:
- ~6 ad-hoc check scripts at repo root (not in `scripts/`)
- Created during Phase 0 WIP triage
- Jeff hasn't decided whether to commit / archive / delete

**Recommendation per file:**
1. If recurring/multi-use → promote to `server/services/skills/<name>.ts` admin endpoint
2. If one-shot diagnostic → move to `scripts/_archive/2026-Q1/`
3. If dangerous (touches DB) → delete + replace with admin procedure

Without seeing file contents, can't sort each individually. **Jeff decision needed: drop the stash or apply + commit then sort.**

### scripts/ count

`ls scripts/*.{mjs,ts} | wc -l` → **93 files** (v1 audit said 102 — already 9 removed in v1).

Sample categorization:
- 8 `fix-*` files (`fix-category.mjs`, `fix-color-theme.mjs`, `fix-flights.mjs`, `fix-router.py`, `fix-taiwan-tour-*.mjs` × 4, `fix-tour-data.mjs`, `fix-train-name.mjs`) — band-aids, **archive**
- 1 `r67-*` file (was 3 — partially cleaned)
- 3 `japan-sept-*` files — Jeff's identified anti-pattern, **archive**
- 3 `apply-migration-0033/0034/0035.mjs` — should be in `drizzle/`, but `0033/0034/0035` are already in `drizzle/`, so these are stale, **delete**
- ~20 `check-*` and `inspect-*` files — diagnostics, **promote to skill or archive**
- ~10 `cleanup-*`, `purge-*`, `wipe-*` files — dangerous, **delete or replace with admin endpoint**

### Drizzle journal drift

- `drizzle/meta/_journal.json` entries: **75**
- `drizzle/*.sql` files: **76** (one extra)
- Plus 2 in `drizzle/_deferred/` (0060, 0061 — cancel-pair noted in completion report)
- 1 `.down.sql` (0076)

Journal vs SQL file count differs by 1 — likely the `.down.sql` is not in journal (correct). Or one of the SQL files isn't in journal yet. Worth manual confirmation but **probably benign**.

### Recommended work

| Item | Est. AI hours | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| Write + apply migration 0077_inquiry_type_emergency.sql; remove cast | 1 | P1 |
| Triage 6 stashed root-check scripts (Jeff 30-min decision; then mechanical) | 2 (after Jeff) | P1 |
| Archive `scripts/fix-*` + `scripts/japan-sept-*` (~12 files) to `scripts/_archive/2026-Q1/` | 1 | P1 |
| Delete 3 stale apply-migration-0033/4/5 + delete `wipe-all-tours.mjs` + replace with admin proc | 3 | P1 |
| Promote `i18n-audit.mjs`, `mobile-audit.mjs`, `inspect-recent-tours.mjs`, `llm_dashboard.mjs` to admin endpoints | 8 | P2 |
| Verify journal-vs-file count + reconcile if drifted | 1 | P2 |

**Domain total: ~16 hours; P1 alone = ~7 hours.**

---

## Cross-Cutting Themes

### Theme 1: "Autonomy depth" is the single biggest gap

Domains A, B, and parts of I and F all point to the same hole: **the autonomous loop exists but has no test coverage, no observability, no skill-dispatch wiring, and missing intent vocabulary.** Each of these alone is a problem; together they make the autonomy thesis only theoretically delivered. Concentrating effort on **the autonomy capability stack first** (intents + skill registry + tests + Sentry) gives compounding returns — once you trust the loop, you can scale customer volume without scaling Jeff hours, which is the entire point.

### Theme 2: Customer experience vs admin experience split

- Customer side: 640 i18n leaks + customer-facing god-file (TourDetailPeony) + admin preview-customer pixel drift + Admin.js bundling hurting Jeff's mobile admin time
- Admin side: 506 i18n leaks (Jeff reads them so lower priority) + 33 unsplit routers (admin tabs fine if individually small) + missing admin design-system primitives

**P0 work should weight customer-side ~2-3x admin-side.**

### Theme 3: v1 hardened money, v2 needs to harden agents

v1 made Stripe handlers atomic + idempotent + tested. v2 must do the equivalent for autonomous agents: tested, observable, with explicit failure modes. The same pattern — central idempotency, transaction wrapping, vitest coverage — needs to extend from `stripeWebhook.ts` to every file in `server/agents/autonomous/`.

### Theme 4: "Refactor regret" — One v1 over-engineering

**Phase 4E-bis-1 + 4E-bis-2** kept `toursAdmin.ts` (1,240) and `skills.ts` (1,023) flat with the note "v2 sub-split optional". Given that the rest of v1 went much further down the split tree (40 sub-routers from one file), stopping at "flat-but-big" for these two is inconsistent. Either commit to the split philosophy fully or revisit and decide "these two are exempt because". Right now it's neither — they're left as 1,000+ LOC files that violate CLAUDE.md §九's "300 LOC max" rule. Minor over-engineering (they're working) but a credibility cost on the rule.

---

## Recommended Sequence (P0 → P3)

### P0 — Must do to call it "best-in-class" (~140 AI hours)

**Wave 1 — Foundation (parallel-safe, ~30 hours)**
1. **Sentry + UptimeRobot + pino logger** (Domain F) — 15 hours, $0/mo. Unblocks visibility for everything else.
2. **Code-split Admin.js** (Domain H) — 6 hours. Quick win, huge bundle reduction.
3. **Verify + delete `ComponentShowcase.tsx`** if unmounted (Domain C) — 0.5 hours.
4. **PostHog + 5 key events** (Domain J) — 4 hours. Tells you which other P0s matter most.
5. **Apply migration 0077 emergency enum + remove cast** (Domain K) — 1 hour.
6. **Add `rateLimit` to all 33 admin routers + encrypt passport numbers** (Domain G) — 10 hours.

**Wave 2 — Splits (sequential, ~54 hours)**
7. **Split `db.ts` 3,584 → 11 domain files** (Domain C) — 12 hours. Dependency for #8 and beyond.
8. **Split `TourDetailPeony.tsx` 3,827 → 8 sections** (Domains C + E) — 18 hours.
9. **Split `masterAgent.ts` 3,300 → supervisor + 6 pipelines** (Domain C) — 24 hours.

**Wave 3 — Autonomy thesis (~58 hours)**
10. **Add 5 missing sub-intents + wire skill auto-dispatch** (Domain A) — 20 hours.
11. **Port `packgo-flight-ticket` skill** (Domain B) — 8 hours.
12. **Vitest smoke test per autonomous agent** (Domain A + I) — 16 hours.
13. **Smoke test masterAgent + email templates** (Domain I) — 10 hours.
14. **Fix top 10 customer-facing i18n leaks (640 → ~170)** (Domain D) — 16 hours.

### P1 — High ROI (~80 AI hours)

15. Split `agentRouter.ts` (10h)
16. Split `TourEditDialog.tsx` (12h)
17. Port `packgo-china-visa` + `packgo-tour-confirmation` (14h)
18. Wire `RefundAgent` into Stripe refund webhook (4h)
19. Restructure i18n dictionaries (8h)
20. Add Playwright + 5 customer flow tests (14h)
21. Build admin design-system primitives (16h)
22. Add `loading="lazy"` everywhere + font preload (4h)
23. Fix 3 N+1 queries (3h)
24. Enforce `auditLog` on all admin mutations (8h)
25. AB-390 conversion dashboard tile (3h)
26. Archive `scripts/fix-*` + delete stale migrations (4h)

### P2 — Worthwhile but defer (~50 AI hours)

- Split `email.ts`, `calibrationAgent.ts`, `TourRouteMapCanvas.tsx` (22h)
- Refit top 5 admin tabs to primitives (18h)
- Storybook + visual regression (10h)
- Mobile pass on top 10 customer pages (12h)

### P3 — Nice to have

- Split `toursAdmin.ts` + `skills.ts` (consistency with v1 philosophy)
- Port `packgo-flight-confirmation` + `packgo-social-image`
- Bundle analyzer in CI
- Better Stack ($24/mo) log aggregation
- A/B test booking flow (after analytics)

---

## Stage 2 Readiness Checklist

Per CLAUDE.md §九 Vibe Coding, before any P0 work starts:

### For Wave 1 — Foundation

| Item | Input/decision needed |
|---|---|
| Sentry plan | Free tier (5K/mo) sufficient? Or $26/mo Team for source maps? |
| UptimeRobot | Free tier (50 monitors, 5-min) OK? |
| PostHog | EU or US cloud? Free tier (1M events/mo) OK? |
| Admin code-split | Confirm: all admin tabs lazy-load via `React.lazy()` is acceptable UX? |
| ComponentShowcase | Confirm: delete the file (not mounted anywhere)? |
| Migration 0077 | Apply on next deploy or staging-first? Backfill historical "emergency" rows? |
| Passport encryption | Acceptable migration window (re-encrypt on next visa form submission, vs. one-shot migration)? |

### For Wave 2 — Splits

| Item | Input/decision needed |
|---|---|
| db.ts split | Lock the 11 domain boundaries (tours/bookings/users/payments/visa/inquiries/membership/packpoint/agents/audit/search)? |
| TourDetailPeony split | Move sub-components to `client/src/components/tour-detail/` (existing) or create new `pages/TourDetailPeony/` dir? |
| masterAgent split | Pipeline files in `server/agents/_pipeline/` or `server/agents/pipeline/`? |

### For Wave 3 — Autonomy thesis

| Item | Input/decision needed |
|---|---|
| New sub-intents | Lock the 5 new values: `quote_request` / `flight_inquiry` / `tour_comparison_request` / `visa_inquiry` / `deposit_inquiry`? Or different vocabulary? |
| Skill registry shape | Inline map (`intent → orchestrator function`) or DB-driven (`agentSkillRegistry` table)? Recommend inline for v2; DB-driven for v3 when skill count > 20. |
| Auto-send vs draft | When `confidence ≥ 90` should the system auto-send, or always draft for Jeff review in v2? Recommendation: stay draft-first; auto-send is a separate v3 trust milestone. |
| Test mocks | OK to add `vi.mock("server/_core/llm")` returning deterministic fixtures? Or use `nock`/`msw` for HTTP-level mocking? |

### Stage 2 entry criteria

- [ ] Sentry / UptimeRobot / PostHog accounts created (Jeff action, 30 min)
- [ ] Migration 0077 SQL reviewed by Jeff (5 min)
- [ ] 5 sub-intent values confirmed by Jeff (15 min)
- [ ] Skill registry shape decision: inline vs DB-driven (10 min)
- [ ] Auto-send-vs-draft confidence threshold confirmed (5 min)
- [ ] db.ts 11 domain boundaries confirmed (15 min)
- [ ] Stash `phase0/mod4/root-check-scripts` triaged (30 min — applied or dropped)

**Jeff total Stage 2 prep time: ~2 hours.**

End of v2 audit.
